course_title: Human Molecular Genetics in Health & Disease

learning_outcomes.description: Explain the ethical issues that pertain to genetic testing and to the use of genetic data, including genomics.
Describe and discuss the contribution of genetic differences to predisposition to disease.
Justify that the one gene = one disease model is an oversimplification.
Describe the biochemical and molecular biology processes underpinning genetic disorders.
Apply some the experimental approaches that are used to determine the genetic basis of human phenotypes, including diseases, and to be able to articulate how these methods are employed.
Explain how multiple loci/alleles/genes contribute to multi-factorial diseases or predisposition to disease.
Explain how genetics underpins the development of cancers and developmental disorders.
Explain how epigenetics contributes to human health and disease.
Critically evaluate the scientific literature pertaining to human genetics in health and disease.

assessments.due_date:
Workshop 1 online quiz 19/03/2026 2:00 pm

Workshop 2 online quiz 2/04/2026 2:00 pm

Workshop 3 online quiz 23/04/2026 2:00 pm

Workshop 4 online quiz 7/05/2026 2:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
A series of online MCQ quizzes will assess key concepts introduced during the workshop sessions 1 to 4. Practice quizzes will also be available. Quizzes can be accessed via blackboard any time in the 7 days prior to (and including) the due date. Quizzes must be completed within 15 minutes of beginning the quiz. There is no quiz for Workshop 5.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
You will write a short project report assessing your analyses of the molecular genetics activities and literature introduced during Workshops 1 to 5. The report will be structured around specific questions introduced during each workshop. For workshops 1 to 4 the question(s) will require text answers up to 1 page per workshop (including diagrams), and it is expected that you will need to complete this work after completing the Workshop activities. For Workshop 5 the questions are shorter in nature and should be able to be completed during the workshop contact session.  The whole report should be no longer than 5 pages in length (i.e. 1 page per workshop).

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit through the course Turnitin submission point in Blackboard. Before submitting your assessment item to Turnitin please name your file in the following way. FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way. SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1

assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The on-campus, invigilated examination will test your knowledge of topics covered in Module 1 (Developmental Genetics) and Module 2 (Single Gene Diseases).

More information will be provided during teaching activities and/or on Blackboard. 

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Lectures

There are 3 hours of lecture content per week: a 1 hour lecture on Monday and a 2 hour lecture on Friday. Generally each module will begin with a 2 hour Friday lecture, and include a 1 hour Monday lecture and 2 hour Wednesday lecture the following week. In between modules there are dedicated 1 hour Monday lectures to introduce workshops and provide feedback on assessment items and exam preparation.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 3 To Week 11(09 Mar - 17 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Workshops

There are five 2 hour workshop sessions (BYOD) designed to support learning for selected modules with computer-based exercises and/or discussion-based activities. Each workshop is run twice (Monday and Thursday) - check your timetable to see which session you have been allocated. Regular formative assessment, including automatically marked quizzes, will be used during the workshops to provide feedback on progress. Workshops are also supported by dedicated lectures in the usual Monday lecture slot.

Refer to the detailed timetable on blackboard for further details.
